sources.title: Orchestrate teams of Claude Code sessions - Claude Code Docs
keyInsights: Team lead + teammates architecture: lead coordinates, teammates work independently in own context windows
Mailbox messaging system: JSON inbox files at ~/.claude/teams/{team-name}/ with automatic delivery
Shared task list with file-locking for race-free task claiming
Two display modes: in-process (any terminal) and split-panes (tmux/iTerm2)
Hooks for quality gates: TeammateIdle, TaskCreated, TaskCompleted
Subagent definitions reusable as teammate roles
3-5 teammates recommended; 5-6 tasks per teammate optimal
No session resumption for in-process teammates (known limitation)

sources.title: How we built our multi-agent research system - Anthropic
keyInsights: Orchestrator-worker: lead analyzes queries, spawns subagents for parallel exploration
Lead saves research plan to external memory to survive 200k token context truncation
Effort scaling: simple queries use 1 agent + 3-10 tool calls; complex queries use 10+ subagents
15x more tokens than chats but enables up to 90% research time reduction for complex queries
Prompt engineering over rigid rules: encode human heuristics (decomposition, source evaluation)
Monitor emergent behaviors without reading conversation contents

sources.title: AI Agent Orchestration Patterns - Azure Architecture Center
keyInsights: Sequential: linear pipeline, each agent processes previous output (also: pipeline, prompt chaining)
Concurrent: parallel fan-out/fan-in, all agents work simultaneously then aggregate
Group chat: shared conversation thread, chat manager coordinates, all agents see accumulating context
Handoff: dynamic routing/triage, full control transfers from agent to agent
Maker-checker loop: maker creates, checker evaluates against criteria, iterate until approved
Start with lowest complexity level; add agents only when single-agent fails due to prompt complexity
Limit group chat to 3 agents max to maintain control

sources.title: Multi-agent collaboration - AWS Prescriptive Guidance
keyInsights: Collaboration is peer-to-peer emergent coordination; workflow agents use centralized control
Implementation flow: task initiation → role assignment → agent communication → specialized reasoning → output coordination
Communication channels: shared memory, messaging queues, or prompt chaining
AWS stack: SQS/EventBridge for messaging, DynamoDB/S3 for blackboard state, Step Functions for orchestration

sources.title: Announcing the Agent2Agent Protocol (A2A) - Google Developers Blog
keyInsights: A2A enables agents from different vendors/frameworks to communicate via HTTP + SSE + JSON-RPC
Agent Card: JSON self-description of capabilities, enabling automatic agent discovery
Client agent formulates tasks; remote agent executes; synchronized status through message parts
Messages contain typed parts: text, images, video, interactive elements
Linux Foundation governance as of June 2025; 50+ enterprise partners
Version 0.3 adds gRPC support and signed Agent Cards

sources.title: The Adventuring Party: From Sub-Agents to Multi-Agent Orchestration with tmux
keyInsights: Three adjacent tmux panes: Codex (reviewer), Claude (implementor), Shell (utilities)
tmux send-keys enables asynchronous bidirectional communication with persistent context
Evolution: sub-agent relay → direct CLI (synchronous) → tmux transport (async)
Master-worker: Go TUI displays worker progress, enables message relay and session jumping
Quality gates: RED/GREEN phases, sub-agent critics, deep review, evidence JSON logging
Separate implementor from reviewer; never allow self-approval
Evidence becomes stale; gates force re-review if code changes after approval

sources.title: Tmux Orchestrator - Run AI agents 24/7
keyInsights: Three-tier hierarchy: Orchestrator → Project Managers → Engineers
send-claude-message.sh script: ./send-claude-message.sh session:window 'message'
Sessions survive disconnects: work continues when laptop is closed
Self-scheduling: ./schedule_with_note.sh 30 'Continue implementation'
Git safety: feature branches + 30-minute commits + stable version tags

sources.title: Building Agent Teams in OpenCode: Architecture of Multi-Agent Coordination
keyInsights: Per-agent JSONL files at team_inbox/<projectId>/<teamName>/<agentName>.jsonl (append-only for O(1))
Fire-and-forget + auto-wake: messages inject as synthetic user messages restarting idle prompt loops
Peer-to-peer: agents message teammates directly, not only through lead
Dual state machines: coarse lifecycle (ready/busy/shutdown) + fine execution (10 states)
Sub-agent isolation via permission deny rules + hidden tool visibility
Recovery: interrupted teammates marked ready but prompt loops don't auto-restart

sources.title: Collaborative Problem-Solving with the Blackboard Architecture
keyInsights: Three components: Blackboard (shared state), Knowledge Sources (agents), Control Component (orchestrator)
Agent condition part (activation criteria) + action part (modifications to blackboard)
Control strategies: priority-based, focus-based, opportunistic
Document analysis example: EntityExtractor, SentimentAnalyzer, SummarySynthesizer with preconditions
Opportunistic reasoning: agents respond to evolving state, not predetermined sequences
Modern implementations: Redis/Kafka for distributed blackboard; LLMs as knowledge sources and control

sources.title: Multi-Agent Communication Patterns That Actually Work
keyInsights: Five patterns: Shared Filesystem (mailbox), Relay Agent (hub-and-spoke), Shared Ledger (event sourcing), Handoff Chain, Contract Board
Mailbox: atomic writes + timestamp naming; best for 2-5 same-machine agents
Relay: central routing + audit trail; best for 5-50 heterogeneous agents across machines
Shared Ledger: append-only log, full auditability; needs log compaction
Handoff Chain: structured context transfer; only for serial workflows
Anti-patterns: free-form text parsing, omniscient hub, polling storms, unsequenced delivery
Start simple: Mailbox + Handoff Chain covers most use cases

sources.title: Multi-Agent Memory from a Computer Architecture Perspective
keyInsights: Three-layer memory hierarchy: I/O (interfaces), Cache (fast/limited), Memory (persistent/retrieval)
Shared memory: common pool, easy reuse, requires coherence; distributed: local + selective sync
Without coherence: agents overwrite each other, read stale info, or use inconsistent versions
Missing protocols: no principled KV cache sharing across agents; no memory access permission spec
Read-time conflicts and update-time visibility are the key unsolved consistency challenges
Agent performance is an end-to-end data movement problem

sources.title: Multi-Agent Coordination Gone Wrong? Fix With 10 Strategies
keyInsights: Coordination failures = 36.9% of all multi-agent failures; framework failure rates: 41-86.7%

10 strategies: deterministic task allocation, hierarchical decomposition, token budgets/timeouts, shared memory with ACLs, consistency checks, contention detection, consensus voting, guardrails, CLHF, checkpoint rollbacks
Byzantine Fault-Tolerant consensus reduces attack success from 46.34% to 19.37%
Coordination gains plateau beyond 4 agents
Error amplification 17x in decentralized 'bag of agents' vs. centralized control plane

sources.title: 8 Tactics to Reduce Context Drift with Parallel AI Agents
keyInsights: Central Shared Task Spec: single living document as source of truth for all agents
Scoped Role-Specific Context: each agent receives only relevant code/rules
External Memory with Selective Retrieval: knowledge base queried per task, not embedded in chat history
Aggressive History Trimming: condense exchanges into precise decision notes
Coordinator/Arbiter Agent: meta-agent monitors parallel workers, flags spec conflicts
Structured State and Protocols: explicit fields + procedural steps (plan/propose/review/finalize)
Guardrail Prompts: hard limits on security/compliance enforced via linters/tests
Observability: log prompts + context + decisions; connect metrics to business outcomes

sources.title: Four Design Patterns for Event-Driven, Multi-Agent Systems
keyInsights: Orchestrator-Worker via Kafka: key-based topic partitioning distributes tasks without connection management
Hierarchical Agent: same event-driven techniques apply recursively per subtree
Blackboard via Kafka: shared topic as async knowledge repository, no direct agent communication
Market-Based: bid/ask topics with market maker, eliminates quadratic agent connections
Consumer group rebalancing: auto load distribution when agents added/removed
Immutable event logs: single source of truth, enables replay and recovery

sources.title: CrewAI vs LangGraph vs AutoGen: Choosing the Right Framework
keyInsights: LangGraph: graph-based workflows, typed state channels, conditional edges, LangSmith debugging
CrewAI: role-based team model, task.context[] for state sharing, allow_delegation for peer delegation
AutoGen: group chat model, dynamic conversational collaboration, flexible agent topologies
Without shared visual reference, parallel agents diverge on different data and make contradictory decisions
CrewAI hierarchical process needs manager_llm + memory=True to cache context across delegations

sources.title: Collaboration - CrewAI Docs
keyInsights: allow_delegation=True gives agents delegate_work and ask_question tools automatically
allowed_agents parameter constrains delegation to specific subordinates
context=[task] parameter threads prior task outputs into dependent tasks
step_callback tracks delegation events: 'Delegate work to coworker', 'Ask question to coworker'
memory=True reduces manager re-read cost ~30% by caching delegation context

sources.title: Agent2Agent (A2A) GitHub Repository
keyInsights: Open protocol for communication between opaque agentic applications
Specification at https://a2a-protocol.org/latest/specification/
Uses HTTP, SSE, JSON-RPC as transport
Agent Cards in JSON advertise capabilities for discovery
Task lifecycle: submitted → running → completed/failed/cancelled
Python and JavaScript SDKs available

sources.title: MCP Agent Mail: Coordination Infrastructure for AI Coding Agents
keyInsights: Persistent identities: memorable adjective+noun names registered per project
Git-backed JSONL + SQLite FTS5: messages stored outside agent context windows
File reservation leases: advisory/exclusive on glob patterns with TTL expiration
Pre-commit hook enforcement: prevents simultaneous conflicting edits
Multi-repo coordination via request_contact/respond_contact handshakes
Web UI for human oversight of projects, messages, reservations

sources.title: Multi-agent systems: Why coordinated AI beats going solo
keyInsights: Shared memory architectures with proper access controls prevent stale-information failures
Redis as blackboard: agents never forget history, never overwrite each other with proper boundaries
Vector search enables semantic retrieval across agent-written knowledge
TTL-based expiration keeps shared state fresh without manual cleanup

sources.title: Claude Code --teammate-mode Explained: Run Multiple Agents That Actually Collaborate
keyInsights: CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 environment variable or settings.json to enable
--teammate-mode in-process flag forces single-terminal mode
Shift+Down cycles through teammates; Ctrl+T toggles task list
Teammates load CLAUDE.md automatically from working directory
Claude proposes a team if it determines parallel work would benefit the task

sources.title: OpenAI Swarm: Educational Multi-Agent Orchestration Framework
keyInsights: Two primitives: Agents (LLM + instructions + tools) and handoffs (transfer control to another agent)
Stateless client-side orchestration: conversation history passed between calls, no server state
Handoffs carry conversation context through transitions
Triage pattern: receiving agent classifies intent and hands off to specialist
Superseded by OpenAI Agents SDK (released March 2026) for production use

sources.title: Hierarchical Agent Teams - LangGraph
keyInsights: Top-level supervisor + mid-level supervisors + workers compose via subgraph nesting
Typed state channels: each node reads/writes well-defined state schema
Conditional edges route to next agent based on current state
LangSmith integration enables step-by-step debugging of agent decisions
Structured output for supervisor routing decisions (2026 best practice)

sources.title: Why Multi-Agent LLM Systems Fail: Key Issues Explained
keyInsights: Under-specification causes 15% of breakdowns (MAST taxonomy)
Prompt injection (OWASP LLM01:2025) has 46% baseline success rate in multi-agent systems
Memory poisoning: malicious outputs propagate through shared state to other agents
Circular dependencies: agents deadlock in recursive planning loops
Tool misuse: compromised agents accessing unauthorized resources

sources.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration Patterns with Strands Agents
keyInsights: Swarm pattern: peer agents exchange directly, each approaches from different perspective
No central controller in swarm; intelligence emerges from collective sharing and refinement
Handoff collaboration: agents know about each other and decide when to defer
Agent-as-tool: orchestrator calls agents as if they were function tools, single control thread
Amazon Strands framework supports all three patterns natively
